# Research - 2026-06-30

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> 2-day delta. Tuesday -> weekly repos (claude-code, aider)
> skipped. Three NEW items, all bug fixes against shipped code.
> Prior brief #1 (compaction) and #3 (per-model `/cost today`)
> MERGED (`6d885fdb`, `a5066c37`); #2 (`formatProviderError`) still
> open -- ship first. #1 below is the most direct: an MCP server
> sending both `content` AND `structuredContent` has its rich
> content silently dropped -- the bug opencode #34505 fixed
> yesterday. cline #11984 (Hub recovery) and #11990 (ClinePass)
> are Cline-Hub-specific; opencode #34547/#34352 are desktop-only.

## Executive Summary

- **#1 opencode #34505 (~1h)**: `src/mcp/client.ts:509` prefers
  `structuredContent` over `content`, dropping images / resource
  links when servers send both. Return `content` when non-empty;
  fall back to `structuredContent` flatten only when empty.
- **#2 opencode #34467 (~2h)**: `provider.complete()` in
  `orchestrator.ts:78` ignores AbortSignal -- Ctrl+C in
  non-streaming `sendChat` cannot cancel an in-flight SAP request.
  Mirror `streamingOrchestrator`'s `signal?: AbortSignal`.
- **#3 kilocode #11621 (~2h)**: `task.ts` early-return errors
  (`:192`, `:202`) drop `taskId` so a parent cannot resume a failed
  subagent. Surface `taskId` whenever the task row exists.
- **Cadence**: aider 39 days dry (last 2026-05-22), claude-code 9
  cycles dry. Retag both `<!-- monthly (1st Mon) -->`.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. MCP: preserve content when structuredContent is also present (tool, ~1h)

- **What**: In `src/mcp/client.ts:505-516`, invert priority. If
  `result.content` is non-empty, run the existing
  `content.map(formatContentPart).join('\n')` path (`:523-530`).
  Fall through to `structuredContent` stringify only when `content`
  is empty/missing. Rewrite the leading comment: spec treats
  `content` as canonical, `structuredContent` as supplementary.
  Tests: `{content:[{type:'image',...}], structuredContent:{...}}`
  returns formatted image text, not JSON; empty `content` +
  `structuredContent` still returns the stringified object.
- **Why**: Screenshot / file-resource MCP tools (Playwright, Qt
  Creator, Codex wrappers) routinely send both. Our order is what
  opencode shipped on 2026-06-08 and reverted yesterday after
  Playwright screenshots came through as opaque JSON.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#34505 (commit `fd213e6`, 2026-06-29,
  `+49 -1`, `packages/opencode/src/mcp/catalog.ts`).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~1h).

#### 2. AbortSignal in non-streaming sendChat (provider, ~2h)

- **What**: Add `signal?: AbortSignal` to `sendChat` options
  (`orchestrator.ts:6-14`), forward as `provider.complete(messages,
  { maxTokens: 4096, signal })` (`:78`). `complete` (`:791`)
  already accepts `CompletionOptions` but does NOT pass `signal`
  to `client.chatCompletion` -- thread it through the
  `requestConfig.signal` SAP SDK option (the streaming path on
  `:879` already does this). Wire `interactive.ts` Ctrl+C handler
  to abort the controller for non-streaming sends. Tests: aborting
  mid-call rejects with `AbortError`; `recordRouteOutcome` is NOT
  called for aborted requests.
- **Why**: With non-streaming routing (short prompts, summarizer)
  Ctrl+C cosmetically returns control but SAP keeps running and
  billing. Streaming has the signal, non-streaming does not.
- **Source**: sst/opencode#34467 (commit `884c256`, 2026-06-29,
  `+19 -1`, `packages/console/app/src/routes/zen/util/handler.ts`).
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~2h).

#### 3. Surface taskId on subagent failure paths (tool, ~2h)

- **What**: In `src/tool/tools/task.ts`, every `return { success:
  false, error: ... }` AFTER `taskId` is bound (`:209`) must
  include `data: { taskId, status: 'failed' }` -- specifically
  `:202-205` (validate failure) and the `queueBackgroundTask`
  `.catch` (`:268-272`) where `taskData.error` is set but no
  surface exists. Update `:152` so callers know to pass the
  returned `taskId` back as `task_id`. Tests: validate failure
  returns `taskId`; background failure is retrievable via
  `task_status` with `status:'failed'` + `error`.
- **Why**: A subagent that fails before output is one-shot -- the
  parent never sees the id, so `task_id` resume (`:14`, `:152`)
  cannot recover. Store already retains the row (`:221`).
- **Source**: Kilo-Org/kilocode#11621 (merge `4a8f324`, 2026-06-30,
  `+96 -4`, `packages/opencode/src/tool/task.ts`).
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: small (~2h).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Order**: ship prior brief's #2 (`formatProviderError`, ~2h,
   still open) FIRST, then #1 here (1h, highest user impact), then
   #2 (2h, wires existing infra), then #3 (2h, tool contract).
2. **Demote aider + claude-code to monthly**: aider 39 days dry,
   claude-code 9 cycles dry. Retag in
   `.github/prompts/agent1-research-system.md` and the workflow
   task prompt to `<!-- monthly (1st Mon) -->`.
3. **Defer**: cline #11984 (`loadSession` already null-safe);
   opencode #34521 (no models.dev); kilocode #11782 (vscode-only).
